# DarkSlide v1.1.0

DarkSlide 1.1.0 is a quality-and-hardening release. Sixteen-bit exports now keep their tonal resolution through the entire adjustment pipeline, a new Auto White Balance control neutralizes casts in one click, borderless scans get a proper film-base estimate, and every fixable dependency security alert has been cleared.

## Highlights

- True 16-bit export fidelity: the float pipeline now uses 4096-entry curve tables with linear interpolation instead of 256-entry 8-bit LUTs, so 16-bit exports keep full tonal resolution through the curve stage.
- Float-precision sharpen and noise-reduction variants remove intermediate 8-bit rounding, so 16-bit exports honor spatial settings end to end.
- New White Balance sidebar section with an Auto WB button that applies only temperature and tint from the existing neutral-balance analysis.
- Borderless scans are covered by a new in-frame film-base estimator that picks the brightest low-texture interior patch when the border band shows no film rebate, with capped low-confidence provenance ("in-frame / auto-estimated-in-frame") recorded in the film-base decision record.
- Security hardening across the whole stack: all fixable Dependabot alerts cleared on npm and Cargo, including the Tauri 2.11.1 origin-confusion fix and updates to rustls-webpki, the JPEG XL decoder family, tar, and serde_with — every bump staying within current major versions.

## Why This Matters

High-bit-depth TIFF exports previously funneled through 8-bit lookup tables at the curve and spatial stages, quietly quantizing tones that 16-bit delivery is meant to preserve. The reworked float path keeps exports smooth for print-grade output. Meanwhile, scans without a visible film rebate used to fall back to guesswork for the orange-mask base; the in-frame estimator gives them a measured, clearly-labeled starting point instead.

## Desktop Downloads

GitHub Actions builds and attaches installers for each supported desktop platform:

- macOS: universal Apple Silicon and Intel `.dmg`
- Windows: `.msi` and NSIS `.exe`
- Linux: `.deb` and `.AppImage`

The desktop app adds RAW import, native dialogs, file associations, scanning sessions, external editor handoff, and local update checks. The browser app remains available for JPEG, PNG, TIFF, and WebP workflows without installing anything.

## Known Notes

- macOS builds are not notarized. On first launch, macOS may require opening the app through System Settings > Privacy & Security > Open Anyway.
- Windows and Linux builds are unsigned.
- RAW support is available in the desktop app and remains an advanced workflow; JPEG, PNG, TIFF, and WebP are the stable browser-supported formats.
- Four dependency advisories remain open with no patched release or no in-major fix (quick-xml, crossbeam-epoch, glib, rand 0.7); none are reachable with untrusted input in DarkSlide's local-first workflow. Details are documented in PR #3.

## Checks

This release is built by GitHub Actions on macOS, Windows, and Linux. The release workflow also keeps the generated installers as workflow artifacts so they can be inspected even if a release upload has to be retried.
